title: reseda alba var. propinqua
intro: Reseda alba var. propinqua, also known as White Mignonette, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Boraginaceae, characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities. Reseda alba var. propinqua has been reported to have various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Reseda alba var. propinqua include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
